mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack triggers corporate earnings revisions downward, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal a synchronized demand contraction that forces equity market repricing downward over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC below -0.03, or positive returns occur in >55% of co-declining months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, a contraction in the index of industrial production indicates supply-side bottlenecks that reduce corporate profit margins and drive negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS growth falls below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year rolling window, or negative returns dominate in >58% of high-growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 295, aggressive central bank policy expectations trigger immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 295, or the 5-day forward return distribution shows no significant negative skew.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling household formation rates indicates structural demand weakness that suppresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-HOUST divergence yields a Rank IC above -0.02, or positive quarterly returns occur in >60% of divergent periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a recovering labor market, a sharp drop in industrial production signals an impending recessionary shock that forces immediate negative equity repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during low unemployment periods fail to predict negative returns (Rank IC > -0.03), or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% of triggered instances.
